---
title: "Vienna, Bratislava & Budapest"
subtitle: "Vienna · Bratislava · Budapest"
page-layout: full
toc: false
sidebar: false
---

```{=html}
<style>
  .trip-header {
    text-align: center;
    padding: 60px 20px 40px;
    max-width: 860px;
    margin: 0 auto;
  }
  .trip-label {
    font-size: 0.75rem;
    letter-spacing: 0.2em;
    text-transform: uppercase;
    color: #9b8877;
    margin-bottom: 8px;
  }
  .trip-title {
    font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', sans-serif;
    font-size: 2.6rem;
    font-weight: 800;
    color: #1E4264;
    margin: 0 0 12px;
    line-height: 1.1;
  }
  .trip-subtitle {
    font-family: 'Cormorant Garamond', serif;
    font-style: italic;
    font-size: 1.2rem;
    color: #6b7280;
    margin-bottom: 24px;
  }
  .trip-meta {
    display: flex;
    gap: 24px;
    justify-content: center;
    font-size: 0.8rem;
    color: #9b8877;
    letter-spacing: 0.06em;
    text-transform: uppercase;
    flex-wrap: wrap;
  }
  .section-divider {
    border: none;
    border-top: 1px solid #e0e0f0;
    margin: 48px auto;
    max-width: 600px;
  }
  .prose {
    max-width: 720px;
    margin: 0 auto;
    font-size: 1.05rem;
    line-height: 1.85;
    color: #2d3748;
  }
  .prose h2 {
    font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', sans-serif;
    font-weight: 800;
    font-size: 1.6rem;
    color: #1E4264;
    margin-top: 56px;
    margin-bottom: 12px;
    text-align: center;
  }
  .prose p {
    margin-bottom: 1.4rem;
  }
  .media-block {
    max-width: 860px;
    margin: 32px auto;
    border-radius: 14px;
    overflow: hidden;
    box-shadow: 0 4px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.10);
  }
  .media-block img,
  .media-block video {
    width: 100%;
    display: block;
    object-fit: cover;
  }
  .media-caption {
    font-family: 'Cormorant Garamond', serif;
    font-style: italic;
    font-size: 0.9rem;
    color: #9b8877;
    text-align: center;
    margin-top: 10px;
    padding: 0 12px 4px;
  }
  .pull-quote {
    font-family: 'Cormorant Garamond', serif;
    font-style: italic;
    font-size: 1.55rem;
    font-weight: 600;
    color: #1E4264;
    text-align: center;
    max-width: 640px;
    margin: 48px auto;
    line-height: 1.5;
    padding: 0 24px;
    border-left: 3px solid #c9aa96;
  }

  #quarto-document-content {
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
    align-items: center;
  }

  #quarto-document-content > * {
    width: 100%;
    max-width: 860px;
  }
</style>

<div class="trip-header">
  <p class="trip-label">🏰 Travel</p>
  <h1 class="trip-title">Three Capitals on the Danube</h1>
  <p class="trip-subtitle">Six days across Vienna, Bratislava and Budapest</p>
  <div class="trip-meta">
    <span>June 2023</span>
    <span>📍 Austria, Slovakia & Hungary</span>
    <span>✈️ 6 days</span>
  </div>
</div>

<hr class="section-divider">
```

<div id="vienna"></div>
::: {.prose}

## Vienna — Elegance as a Default Setting

Vienna operates at a level of architectural consistency that becomes quietly overwhelming after a day or two. Every street seems to have been designed with the understanding that someone would eventually be looking at it, and the effect is cumulative — by the time you've walked past the fourth or fifth monumental building in an hour, you start to understand why the city has the reputation it does.

The palaces and churches set the tone, but it's the streets themselves that sustain it — narrow lanes flanked by tall, ornate buildings that make the city feel like it was built at a scale just slightly larger than necessary, which turns out to be exactly the right scale. The Prater amusement park offers a useful counterpoint: the old Ferris wheel, in particular, is a city landmark that earns its place on the skyline.

The Wiener Schnitzel is non-negotiable. Order it properly — veal, thin, fried in clarified butter, lemon on the side — and it tastes like the city tastes: rich, considered, slightly formal, and entirely worth it.

:::

<div style="display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr; gap: 1rem; margin: 2rem 0;">
  <figure style="margin: 0;">
    <img src="vienna_church.jpg" alt="Gothic church in Vienna"
         style="width: 100%; height: 380px; object-fit: cover; border-radius: 6px;" />
    <figcaption class="media-caption">Gothic stonework — Vienna's churches competing quietly for attention.</figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure style="margin: 0;">
    <img src="vienna_dome.jpg" alt="Inside of an elegant dome in Vienna"
         style="width: 100%; height: 380px; object-fit: cover; border-radius: 6px;" />
    <figcaption class="media-caption">Inside the dome — the kind of interior that makes you stand still.</figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure style="margin: 0;">
    <img src="vienna_statue.jpg" alt="Golden statue in Vienna"
         style="width: 100%; height: 380px; object-fit: cover; border-radius: 6px;" />
    <figcaption class="media-caption">Gold leaf and ornament — Vienna's decorative vocabulary in one statue.</figcaption>
  </figure>
</div>

<div style="display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 1rem; margin: 2rem 0;">
  <figure style="margin: 0;">
    <img src="vienna_scene1.jpg" alt="Magnificent building exterior in Vienna"
         style="width: 100%; height: 440px; object-fit: cover; border-radius: 6px;" />
    <figcaption class="media-caption">Vienna at its most characteristic — grand, considered, built to last.</figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure style="margin: 0;">
    <img src="vienna_scene2.jpg" alt="Sunset over a domed building in Vienna"
         style="width: 100%; height: 440px; object-fit: cover; border-radius: 6px;" />
    <figcaption class="media-caption">The pink sky at sunset — the city at its most theatrical.</figcaption>
  </figure>
</div>

<div style="display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr; gap: 1rem; margin: 2rem 0;">
  <figure style="margin: 0;">
    <img src="vienna_street1.jpg" alt="Narrow street with tall buildings in Vienna"
         style="width: 100%; height: 360px; object-fit: cover; border-radius: 6px;" />
    <figcaption class="media-caption">The narrow streets — tall buildings on both sides, everywhere you turn.</figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure style="margin: 0;">
    <img src="vienna_street2.jpg" alt="Central street in Vienna"
         style="width: 100%; height: 360px; object-fit: cover; border-radius: 6px;" />
    <figcaption class="media-caption">The centre — built at a scale slightly larger than necessary, which is exactly right.</figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure style="margin: 0;">
    <img src="vienna_schnitzel.jpg" alt="Wiener Schnitzel"
         style="width: 100%; height: 360px; object-fit: cover; border-radius: 6px;" />
    <figcaption class="media-caption">The Wiener Schnitzel. Non-negotiable.</figcaption>
  </figure>
</div>

<div class="pull-quote">
  "Every street seems to have been designed with the understanding that someone would eventually be looking at it."
</div>

<hr class="section-divider">

<div id="bratislava"></div>
::: {.prose}

## Bratislava — The Castle on the Hill

Bratislava is an easy day trip from Vienna — an hour by train — and the contrast is instructive. Where Vienna is grand and self-certain, Bratislava is smaller and livelier in the evenings, its old town full of small bars and cafés that fill up as the sun goes down. It is a city that operates at a more human scale and is the better for it.

The castle sits on a hill above the city overlooking the Danube, and the view from the gardens explains immediately why someone chose to build there. The chimney cake — kürtőskalács, a spiral of sweet dough cooked over charcoal — is the essential street food purchase and is best eaten immediately, warm, from the paper bag.

:::

<div class="media-block">
  <img src="bratislava_castle.jpg" alt="Bratislava Castle gardens"
       style="width: 100%; max-height: 500px; object-fit: cover; border-radius: 6px;" />
  <p class="media-caption">Bratislava Castle — sitting on the hill above the Danube, the view earning its place.</p>
</div>

<div style="display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr; gap: 1rem; margin: 2rem 0;">
  <figure style="margin: 0;">
    <img src="bratislava_mecastle.jpg" alt="In the castle gardens at Bratislava"
         style="width: 100%; height: 360px; object-fit: cover; border-radius: 6px;" />
    <figcaption class="media-caption">The castle gardens — worth the climb.</figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure style="margin: 0;">
    <img src="bratislava_church.jpg" alt="Church in Bratislava"
         style="width: 100%; height: 360px; object-fit: cover; border-radius: 6px;" />
    <figcaption class="media-caption">Bratislava's old town — more charm than the city gets credit for.</figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure style="margin: 0;">
    <img src="bratislava_chimney.jpg" alt="Chimney cake in Bratislava"
         style="width: 100%; height: 360px; object-fit: cover; border-radius: 6px;" />
    <figcaption class="media-caption">Chimney cake — eat it warm, immediately, from the bag.</figcaption>
  </figure>
</div>

<hr class="section-divider">

<div id="budapest"></div>
::: {.prose}

## Budapest — The One That Has Everything

Budapest is the kind of city that makes you feel slightly guilty for not having come sooner. Three days is not enough. St. Stephen's Basilica stops you in your tracks from the outside and then delivers again on the inside — the Holy Right Hand of St. Stephen in its reliquary is one of those things that sounds strange until you're standing in front of it. The Parliament Building is one of the most beautiful in the world, full stop: from the Buda side of the Danube it looks like something that shouldn't exist, and the interior — painted ceilings, gilded everything — confirms the exterior was not overselling it.

The Buda side repays the walk across: the Citadel, Buda Castle, and Fisherman's Bastion offer successive vantage points over the city, and the Bastion itself is architectural theatre — turrets and terraces and sweeping views of the Parliament across the water. The caves under Budapest are something else entirely: a network of passages and chambers formed by thermal springs, running beneath the city in a way that makes the surface feel like a different world.

The nightlife is legendary for good reason. The ruin bars — Instant, Fogas, the whole complex — are a Budapest invention: former industrial and residential buildings taken over and turned into multi-room venues that work on a logic that only makes sense once you're inside them. The food is cheap and good. Borzas borda and a cheap beer is the right order. The Pinball Museum is exactly what it sounds like and better than it has any right to be — a room full of working machines from every decade, all included in the entry price.

:::

<div style="display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 1rem; margin: 2rem 0;">
  <figure style="margin: 0;">
    <img src="budapest_parliament1.jpg" alt="Budapest Parliament Building"
         style="width: 100%; height: 440px; object-fit: cover; border-radius: 6px;" />
    <figcaption class="media-caption">The Parliament Building — one of the most beautiful in the world, from the outside.</figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure style="margin: 0;">
    <img src="budapest_parliament2.jpg" alt="Budapest Parliament from across the river"
         style="width: 100%; height: 440px; object-fit: cover; border-radius: 6px;" />
    <figcaption class="media-caption">From the Buda side — looking like something that shouldn't exist.</figcaption>
  </figure>
</div>

<div class="media-block">
  <img src="budapest_parliamentin.jpg" alt="Inside the Budapest Parliament, gilded ceiling"
       style="width: 100%; max-height: 520px; object-fit: cover; border-radius: 6px;" />
  <p class="media-caption">Inside the Parliament — painted ceilings, gilded everything, the interior matching the exterior's promise.</p>
</div>

<div style="display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 1rem; margin: 2rem 0;">
  <figure style="margin: 0;">
    <img src="budapest_bastion1.jpg" alt="Fisherman's Bastion Budapest"
         style="width: 100%; height: 420px; object-fit: cover; border-radius: 6px;" />
    <figcaption class="media-caption">Fisherman's Bastion — turrets, terraces, and views of the Parliament across the water.</figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure style="margin: 0;">
    <img src="budapest_bastion2.jpg" alt="Fisherman's Bastion statue"
         style="width: 100%; height: 420px; object-fit: cover; border-radius: 6px;" />
    <figcaption class="media-caption">The statue at the Bastion — architectural theatre on the Buda side.</figcaption>
  </figure>
</div>

<div style="display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 1rem; margin: 2rem 0;">
  <figure style="margin: 0;">
    <img src="budapest_panoramic.jpg" alt="Panoramic view from the top of St Stephen's Basilica"
         style="width: 100%; height: 420px; object-fit: cover; border-radius: 6px;" />
    <figcaption class="media-caption">From the top of the Basilica — Budapest laid out in every direction.</figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure style="margin: 0;">
    <img src="budapest_hand.jpg" alt="Holy Right Hand of St Stephen in Budapest"
         style="width: 100%; height: 420px; object-fit: cover; border-radius: 6px;" />
    <figcaption class="media-caption">The Holy Right Hand of St. Stephen — sounds strange until you're standing in front of it.</figcaption>
  </figure>
</div>

<div style="display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 1rem; margin: 2rem 0;">
  <figure style="margin: 0;">
    <img src="budapest_cave.jpg" alt="Caves under Budapest"
         style="width: 100%; height: 420px; object-fit: cover; border-radius: 6px;" />
    <figcaption class="media-caption">The caves beneath the city — thermal springs, passages, a different world entirely.</figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure style="margin: 0;">
    <img src="budapest_caveme.jpg" alt="In the caves under Budapest"
         style="width: 100%; height: 420px; object-fit: cover; border-radius: 6px;" />
    <figcaption class="media-caption">Underground Budapest — the surface feels far away down here.</figcaption>
  </figure>
</div>

<div style="display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 1rem; margin: 2rem 0;">
  <figure style="margin: 0;">
    <img src="budapest_food.jpg" alt="Borzas borda in Budapest"
         style="width: 100%; height: 380px; object-fit: cover; border-radius: 6px;" />
    <figcaption class="media-caption">Borzas borda — the right order, at the right price.</figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure style="margin: 0;">
    <img src="budapest_pinball.jpg" alt="Pinball Museum Budapest"
         style="width: 100%; height: 380px; object-fit: cover; border-radius: 6px;" />
    <figcaption class="media-caption">The Pinball Museum — better than it has any right to be.</figcaption>
  </figure>
</div>

<div class="pull-quote">
  "The kind of city that makes you feel slightly guilty for not having come sooner."
</div>

<hr class="section-divider">

::: {.prose}

Three cities, six days, all on the Danube. The river connects them in more than geography — there's a shared scale, a shared sense that these cities were built for something, and a shared appetite for the evening hours that makes all three worth staying in past dark.

Vienna is the one with the reputation and it earns it. Bratislava is the one that surprises you. Budapest is the one that makes you want to stay longer than you planned and come back sooner than you expect.

:::

<div class="media-block">
  <img src="budapest_parliament2.jpg" alt="Budapest Parliament at night across the Danube"
       style="width: 100%; max-height: 620px; object-fit: cover; border-radius: 6px;" />
  <p class="media-caption">Budapest from across the Danube — the one that has everything.</p>
</div>
